author: Austin Crawford
title: Country Hoedown Hootenanny! at Champlin's
summary: Dust off those boots, tip your hats, and kick up the dust! Join us at Trader's Dock Bar for a night of country vibes, good times, and tunes that'll make your boots tap. Grab your friends and two-step your way to a boot-scootin' good time!"
content: Dust off those boots, tip your hats, and kick up the dust! Join us at Trader's Dock Bar for a night of country vibes, good times, and tunes that'll make your boots tap. Grab your friends and two-step your way to a boot-scootin' good time!"

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Arts and Artisans Festival
summary: On the grounds of the Narragansett Inn, 1 Ocean Ave in New Harbor, Block Island. Across from Dead Eye Dick's.
content:
On the grounds of the Narragansett Inn, 1 Ocean Ave in New Harbor, Block Island. Across from Dead Eye Dick's.
Local artists and artisans will display their works for sale. You will find iconic Rhode Island paintings, Block Island photography, custom designed jewelry, children's appliquéd clothing, license plate art, blown and cut glass, fused glass, nostalgia art, seaweed prints, unique Block Island paintings, prints, ornaments, pottery, jewelry and so much more.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Pirate Night at Champlin's
summary: Ahoy, mateys! Prepare to set sail for this exciting Block Island Tradition. Don your finest pirate attire and join us for a night filled with unforgettable memories. Arrr you ready for the adventure of a lifetime? Don't miss out!
content: Ahoy, mateys! Prepare to set sail for this exciting Block Island Tradition. Don your finest pirate attire and join us for a night filled with unforgettable memories. Arrr you ready for the adventure of a lifetime? Don't miss out!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Arts & Craft Guild at Block Island Historical Society
summary: There’s something about an island that attracts artists and craftspeople, and Block Island is no different. Maybe it’s the quality of the light and maybe it’s the more laid-back lifestyle, but the charms of the island find expression in the works created here.
content:
There’s something about an island that attracts artists and craftspeople, and Block Island is no different. Maybe it’s the quality of the light and maybe it’s the more laid-back lifestyle, but the charms of the island find expression in the works created here. 

If you’d like to take home an artistic reminder of beautiful Block Island, one of the best spots to browse and buy takes place outside of the Block Island Historical Society. Several times each summer, from 9 am to 1 pm, the lawn becomes a showplace and marketplace for members of the Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild. Choose among paintings, photographs, sculpture, jewelry, fabric arts, candles, and more. All of the items are handmade by local artists and would make a perfect keepsake for yourself or a gift for the folks back home.

This year, the dates are set for June 28, July 12,  July 26,  August 9,  August 23, September 13, and October 11. There’s no charge for admission to the Fair.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Summer Symphony 2026 at Champlin's
summary: We are thrilled to announce that the Summer Symphony is back! The Block Island Chamber of Commerce is cordially inviting you to join us for a delightful evening of music performed by the talented musicians of the Eastern Connecticut Symphony Orchestra on Thursday, August 13th, 5 to 7 pm. A wonderful performance of the ECSO under the tent at Champlin's Marina & Resort, overlooking Block Island's beautiful Great Salt Pond. With mocktails, cocktails and hors d' oeuvres starting at 5:00pm and performance at 6:00pm. General admission tickets are $55.00 and may be purchased HERE. Please consider sponsoring this very special event.
content: We are thrilled to announce that the Summer Symphony is back! The Block Island Chamber of Commerce is cordially inviting you to join us for a delightful evening of music performed by the talented musicians of the Eastern Connecticut Symphony Orchestra on Thursday, August 13th, 5 to 7 pm. A wonderful performance of the ECSO under the tent at Champlin's Marina & Resort, overlooking Block Island's beautiful Great Salt Pond. With mocktails, cocktails and hors d' oeuvres starting at 5:00pm and performance at 6:00pm. General admission tickets are $55.00 and may be purchased HERE. Please consider sponsoring this very special event.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Mardi Gras Party at Champlin's
summary: Join us for a New England version of Carnival! Stop by the Dock Bar to enjoy the DJ Party from 9:00pm to Midnight. We'll be featuring themed food and beverage specials throughout the day.

author: Austin Crawford
title: 2026 Block Island Half marathon
summary: This race will begin and end at the Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion. The beginning of the race heads north in a flat stretch followed by a gentle uplift to some steeper hills and turns. Runners will see the Great Salt Pond, beach dunes, The North Lighthouse and much more. At the North Light, turn back and get ready for a challenging loop around the beautiful west side of the island. Lots of hills and turns. Register at Active.com. Day-of registration available. Or send us an email recreation@new-shoreham.com
content:
This race will begin and end at the Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion. The beginning of the race heads north in a flat stretch followed by a gentle uplift to some steeper hills and turns. Runners will see the Great Salt Pond, beach dunes, The North Lighthouse and much more. At the North Light, turn back and get ready for a challenging loop around the beautiful west side of the island. Lots of hills and turns. Register at Active.com. Day-of registration available. Or send us an email recreation@new-shoreham.com

 

Click the link to register https://rebrand.ly/adbs8r5

author: Austin Crawford
title: Run Around The Block 15K Road Race
summary: The race, begun by Vin McAloon and Paul Riker in 1975, the Run Around the Block 15K Road Race is one of the oldest races in Rhode Island as well as New England. The timed race is a hilly, winding run along mostly paved roads with a stretch of dirt road that appears twice during the route. It begins at Isaac's Corner near Fresh Pond and ends at Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion
content: The race, begun by Vin McAloon and Paul Riker in 1975, the Run Around the Block 15K Road Race is one of the oldest races in Rhode Island as well as New England. The timed race is a hilly, winding run along mostly paved roads with a stretch of dirt road that appears twice during the route. It begins at Isaac's Corner near Fresh Pond and ends at Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion
address: Isaacs Corner New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Pride 2026
summary: Block Pride returns for its sixth year of queer joy, artistry, nature, and unapologetic celebration on beautiful Block Island. What began as a gathering of friends has grown into a full weekend of coastal magic — honoring our community, uplifting LGBTQ+ voices, and creating space for connection, creativity, and liberation.
content:
Block Pride returns for its sixth year of queer joy, artistry, nature, and unapologetic celebration on beautiful Block Island. What began as a gathering of friends has grown into a full weekend of coastal magic — honoring our community, uplifting LGBTQ+ voices, and creating space for connection, creativity, and liberation.

 

Details on the schedule of events coming soon.

author: Austin Crawford
title: 35th Annual Holiday Shopping Stroll
summary: The Holiday Stroll is a Block Island tradition happening again this year from Friday, November 27th through Sunday, Nov. 29th. Enjoy a weekend - or day - on Block Island, with fun, festivities, and relaxed & friendly shopping.  The Shopping Stroll kicks off the holiday season with the town adorned in festive decorations, a gift basket raffle, and local shops offering incredible sales beginning on Friday morning!

author: Austin Crawford
title: 20th Adrian Mitchel 5K Turkey Trot
summary: Come and enjoy an awesome event on Block Island. This 5K is timed perfectly so you can enjoy the holiday festivities on the island. You can run or walk whichever you choose. The start and finish line is at the Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion on Corn Neck Rd. The race starts at 1:30 pm. Questions? Please call 401-466-3223 ask for Dave or Mac. Or send us a email recreation@new-shoreham.com
content:
Come and enjoy an awesome event on Block Island. This 5K is timed perfectly so you can enjoy the holiday festivities on the island. You can run or walk whichever you choose. Or send us a email recreation@new-shoreham.com

 

Click the link to register https://rebrand.ly/zy6lyuv
